content.claim: Early school leaving rates are sharply higher in least-developed regions, villages, and among Roma populations (58.7% for Roma versus 9.3% for non-Roma in 2023), establishing unequal baseline future options before any closure policy
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Dropout risk is baseline condition independent of closure question; measure does not isolate closure effects
Data aggregates villages nationally without regional differentiation
Causes of differential dropout rates (quality, transportation, cultural, economic) not disaggregated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
